Text 1.
My name is Chloe Stewart.
I was born in the United Kingdom, but I live in the United States now.
I am a photographer and I enjoy taking photos of different weather conditions.
Last year, I spent several months in Australia
and came back to the United States.
I hope to arrange an exhibition of my Australian photos in Soho Photo Gallery in New York.
Taking photos is not very difficult.
What you need is the right camera.
It doesn't need to be expensive or very new.
I've had my old Canon camera since the 1990s.
But I don't usually use a digital camera.
Photos taken by a digital camera look very artificial.

Text 2
Situated where Europe and Asia meet, Istanbul is the largest city and a busy port of Turkey.
Istanbul is full of mosques, markets, palaces, and museums.
The city has a long history, which started more than 2,500 years ago.
One of the city's places of interest is without doubt Ayasollah.
The oldest Christian cathedral.
It was built in the 4th century by the Roman Emperor Constantine.
A thousand years later, after the Turks conquered Istanbul, Mehmed II changed this Christian church into a Muslim mosque.
Then, 500 years after that, in 1935, Ayasofya Cathedral became a museum, which displayed both Christian and Muslim treasures.
The Bosporus Bridge is another of Istanbul's modern tourist attractions.
The Bosporus itself is a narrow passage of water connecting two larger areas of the sea.
The Bosporus Bridge, which is one of several bridges across this narrow passage of water, is the longest bridge in Europe.
The bridge cost around $200 million to build.

Text 3
If you're looking for a festival of culture and color, then the Hindu festival called Holi is for you.
It is liked by many people and is mainly celebrated in India and Nepal, but in recent years the festival has spread to parts of Europe and North America.
During this festival, thousands of Hindus make open-air fires, throw colored powder at each other, and celebrate the beginning of the new season of spring.
During this festival, thousands of Hindus make open-air fires, throw colored powder at each other, and celebrate the beginning of the new season of spring.
The festival is a Sunday festival which starts on the full moon day somewhere between the end of February and the middle of March.
The festival is a Sunday festival which starts on the full moon day somewhere between the end of February and the middle of March.
It is a Hindu religious festival which celebrates the victory of good over evil and the arrival of spring.
However, for many people it is a time to meet friends and play and laugh with them.
The Holi festival has become popular with non-Hindus in some parts of the world.
It is also celebrated as a thanksgiving for a good harvest.

Text 4
In the Soviet period, Georgian cinematography was successfully developing.
Tbilisi Film Studio was known as one of the top three studios in the Soviet Union.
However, Georgian cinematography ceased to exist when the Soviet Union fell to pieces
This was followed by the fall of the Soviet Union.
This was followed by civil war and economic problems.
Because the country was not able to finance films, the industry infrastructure soon came
to an end.
However, little by little, Georgian films and modern Georgian film directors came back
on the scene.
The fact that two Georgian films, Korn Island and Tangerines, were nominated for an Oscar
shows that even with limited finances, it is possible to make world-class films.
Meanwhile, a new generation of Western-educated Georgian filmmakers
began to make films.
Georgian film makers began to develop film in a positive way, while a state-of-the-art
photography industry, in the West, began to appear.
And, these newcomers show that they are good at making feature films that receive positive
reviews.

Text 5
Giant creatures of the prehistoric world, such as dinosaurs, ancient varieties of tigers,
and woolly mammoths, continue to fascinate us. They make us wonder about the state of our world
when these creatures lived on the earth. The woolly mammoths, who were elephant-like creatures,
for instance, lived on the earth even when the Egyptian pharaohs
were in the world. They lived on the earth even when the Egyptian pharaohs were in the world.
busy building the pyramids of Giza. The woolly mammoths are the ancestors of the present-day
Asian elephants. Mammoths are one of the most widely studied animals of prehistoric times.
The drawings of these animals found in ancient caves have allowed scientists to study the
appearance and behavior of these giants. These mammoths were used to living in ice-age conditions.
These giant creatures ate plants, like present-day elephants. Mammoths had short ears and
tails and two layers of fur protecting them against extreme cold. Giant creatures of the

Task 2. You are going to listen to one text with eight questions. Mark the correct answer A, B, or C.
You now have 30 seconds to look through the task. You will then hear the recording twice.
Hello, everybody. I'm Sharon Lovejoy. I'm the author of many books about gardening.
I'd like to talk about how I started writing books about gardening. My book, Make Your Garden
with Sharon Lovejoy, is about how people who enjoy gardening but are unsuccessful at it. As you know,
some people buy some plants and put them in their garden when they move into a new house.
But unfortunately, they are not able to grow trees and flowers in their gardens,
though they use high-quality plants. Why don't they get professional gardeners, you may ask?
Because their services cost a lot of money, and in addition, gardening brings joy to many people.
My readers think I must come from a family of gardeners,
but both of them are not. I'm not a gardener, but I'm a gardener. I'm a gardener, and I'm a gardener.
I'm a gardener, and I'm a gardener. I'm a gardener. I'm a gardener. I'm a gardener. I'm a gardener. I'm a gardener. I'm a gardener. I'm a gardener.
Both my parents are water sports teachers. They did their best to interest me in some kind of sport,
but my poor health did not allow me to do any. Throughout my life, I've worked in different
places. While working for the National Nature Reserve, I was asked to write about my day at
work for an environmental project. I have always been good at writing. I won a short story prize
at school and wrote a couple of interesting essays at college, but I had no real passion
for writing then. Only when I wrote for an environmental project did I learn about the
project I realized how exciting writing could be. So one day, sometime later, I saw an advert in a
magazine. The magazine was looking for a journalist who would write about gardening. I applied
immediately. I thought I could do it parallel with gardening. I got the job, and after a while,
I became an editor. As editors work irregular hours, it didn't leave me time for gardening.
I liked my job, but I loved gardening even more. Consequently, I decided it was time for a change,
and I left the job.
My bosses weren't very happy about it, I must say. So I went back to gardening. At the same time,
I started writing a book. And I've never regretted it, really. My first book was received with
enthusiasm. As a result, I started working on my second book about gardens around the world.
Writing books isn't an easy thing to do. It takes years of experience to learn to write
attractively. In my opinion, my book was a success because I included interviews in a way that amused
the readers. I was able to write a book that was not as easy as it was to write. I was able to
write and write and write. I was able to write a book that was not as easy as it was to write. I was
able to write and write. I was able to write and write. I was able to write and write. I was able
but any writer does the same thing while writing. Today, many magazines and books on gardening
include a lot of photographs, and it's good. But those pictures mostly show how things might be,
and not how they really are. I don't like that. In contrast to those books, my book contains real
life photographs, which brought many people into gardening. The text is still an important part,
though. Readers do not just buy beautiful pictures, but they also buy beautiful pictures.

The idea is to interview the people who have the most amazing gardens in Europe.
